title: JUNIOR MARKETING MANAGER F/H
description:
Rejoindre le Paris Saint-Germain, c’est bien plus qu’intégrer un club sportif.

C’est rejoindre une entreprise à mission, un acteur culturel et social, un collectif de plus de 750 collaborateurs engagés dans plus de 100 métiers, unis par une même ambition : repousser les limites, innover et faire rayonner le Club, sur le terrain comme en coulisses.

Club pluriel et engagé, le PSG regroupe plusieurs disciplines de haut niveau : football, handball, judo, et s’appuie sur un centre de formation intégré, conçu pour transmettre ses valeurs et former la nouvelle génération.

Porté par l’excellence, l’innovation et l’esprit d’équipe, le Paris Saint-Germain est le club de la Nouvelle Génération, où chaque idée compte et où chaque talent peut laisser sa trace.




Description:

Votre terrain de jeu :




Le département Sponsoring du Paris Saint-Germain recherche un(e) Junior Marketing Manager pour renforcer l’équipe Business Development. Sa mission consistera à soutenir l’équipe Business Solutions dans son quotidien, et notamment sur les missions suivantes :




VOS MISSONS :




Structuration de l’inventaire de droits sponsoring

Mise à jour régulière de l’inventaire des droits en fonction des nouveaux partenariats et des évolutions contractuelles des partenaires existants.




Développement d’offres de partenariat et droits marketing associés

Concevoir des recommandations stratégiques et créatives alignées avec le positionnement du Club et les enjeux marketing des prospects ;
Participer à la définition et à la valorisation des droits marketing associés aux offres de partenariat.
Travailler en étroite collaboration avec les équipes commerciales pour adapter les offres aux opportunités business identifiées.




Création de supports de vente innovants

Contribuer à l’amélioration continue des outils et des formats de présentation en lien avec le studio graphique ;
Déployer des supports créatifs et des formats innovants pour renforcer l’impact et la mise en valeur des offres de partenariat.




Ce poste est à pourvoir en CDI, dans nos locaux basés à Boulogne-Billancourt (des déplacements seront à prévoir en France et à l’étranger.)







PROFIL RECHERCHE







Titulaire d'un diplôme de niveau bac+5, vous avez 2 à 3 ans d'expérience dans le sponsoring sportif, le marketing ou en agence.

Compétences :

Anglais courant indispensable (oral et écrit)
Qualités : vous êtes dynamique et créatif ; vous avez une bonne capacité d'analyse et d’esprit de synthèse. Vous savez faire preuve de proactivité et savez travailler en équipe
Vous possédez une très bonne connaissance de l'univers du sport, du lifestyle, de la musique ou de l’art.
Vous avez une appétence prononcée pour la découverte de nouveaux outils et de nouvelles tendances de consommation. Vous avez une bonne culture digitale & social media
Vous avez une très bonne maîtrise du Pack Office (Powerpoint, Excel, Outlook).
Vous avez une bonne compréhension d’un environnement professionnel international




Ensemble, écrivons la suite de l’histoire et construisons l’avenir du Club.

Postulez dès maintenant et rejoignez le club de la Nouvelle Génération.

title: Junior Event Producer
description:
About Us

At Bucket Listers, we're all about unleashing life’s full potential and having a blast along the way! Our goal is to encourage people to make lasting memories, forge new connections, and embark on new adventures through our platform of curated experiences. Our vibrant community of explorers relies on us to help them find the coolest events and experiences any day of the week.

We're on a mission to make magic happen, and we're searching for enthusiastic individuals to join our incredible team. We're not just dreamers; we're seasoned explorers ourselves, and we can't wait to share our infectious positivity and limitless inspiration with the world.




The Opportunity

We’re looking for a highly organized, scrappy, solutions-oriented Junior Event Producer to support the execution of our live experiences from concept to launch. This role works directly under the Senior Project Manager and plays a critical hands-on role in getting events off the ground; managing logistics, coordinating vendors, tracking timelines, and being boots-on-the-ground during installs and openings.

This is not a sideline position; you will be in the details, in the field, and often in the action, especially during launch weeks.




While Bucket Listers is a remote-first company, this position will require the candidate to maintain residency in the eastern time zone.




In this role, you will:

Support end-to-end event production across multiple cities and activations
Assist with vendor coordination (fabricators, AV, F&B partners, staffing agencies, etc.)
Track production timelines, budgets, and deliverables to ensure deadlines are met
Help manage permitting, COI collection, and venue compliance requirements
Coordinate shipments, inventory, and on-site logistics
Build and maintain run-of-show documents and operational playbooks
Support hiring, onboarding, and training of event staff
Travel to event sites for install, launch, and strike
Troubleshoot real-time issues during openings (guest flow, staffing gaps, tech hiccups, vendor delays, etc.)
Be a proactive problem-solver when things inevitably shift




Qualifications:

1–3 years of experience in event production, experiential marketing, hospitality, operations, or relative field.
Experience supporting live event launches or large-scale productions preferred
Extremely resourceful and autonomous in execution
Strong collaborator with excellent relationship-building skills
Strong relationship builder through high levels of customer service, integrity and professionalism.
Extremely organized multi-tasker who can balance multiple projects and priorities at once
Self-starter and highly motivated; capable of working independently and collaboratively within a fast-paced environment
Strong proficiency in Google Suite and project management tools
Willingness to travel 25-50% of the time
Ability to lift up to 40 lbs and work extended hours during installs
Passionate about the Bucket Listers mission




We’re in the business of fun and we are excited to collaborate with people that will add value to our mission and culture.




Bucket Listers is an equal opportunity employer and we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive team.

title: Junior Brand Designer
description:
Book of the Month is seeking a highly creative Jr. Brand Designer to conceptualize, design and produce effective creative deliverables. You will work alongside a talented team of creatives from brief through execution. A successful candidate will have a high impact on executing brand identity, is highly iterative and adaptable, and is fluent in storytelling through animation and motion graphics.

Our brand personality is:


Playfully subversive: A touch rebellious, contrarian, against the grain. We’ve got that in us and we like bringing a bit of it into our brand
Adventurous and Unexpected: We are boldly unconventional with an adventurous spirit that leads us to always try new things
Intelligent: There’s a thread of intelligence that runs through everything we do. Always in combination with the above elements


What you’ll do…


Conceptualize, design and produce high-performing creative assets
Collaborate with creatives and marketers to brainstorm and execute impactful social, digital, and traditional content that reflects BOTM’s brand aesthetic and goals.
Produce high quality content that adheres to our brand’s style guidelines and maintains graphic consistency across all channels, such as our mobile app, web, email, organic and paid social media and print.
Manage multiple projects with tight deadlines, while gathering learnings and insights from previous work to make relevant recommendations moving forward.


What You Should Have…


We are open to candidates with 0-3 years of work experience in graphic design and creative execution
Internship and/or work experience at a consumer-facing brand (preferably DTC) and/or agency is a plus
Strong skill-set in conceptualizing, illustration, graphic design, and animation to create compelling, brand aligned creative.
The ability to effectively collaborate with various teams, providing guidance in your subject matter expertise and a willingness to incorporate industry best practices.
The ability to understand, absorb and execute on creative direction and rounds of feedback
An aptitude for time management, organization, collaboration and direct communication.
Proficient in Figma, Adobe Creative Suite, After Effects, and relevant creative tools
Experience with Blender or other 3D modeling tools is a plus.


Salary Range: $67,000 - $85,000

title: Junior Corporate Recruiter
description:
Bisnow Overview

Bisnow is the leading B2B media platform powering the commercial real estate industry. We connect CRE professionals through award-winning news, high-impact events, marketing solutions, recruiting services, and sales enablement tools that drive real business results.

With 1.7M+ subscribers and 400+ events annually across 47 markets in the U.S., Canada, the UK, Ireland, and the Netherlands, we don’t just cover the industry, we help move it forward.

We inform. We connect. We create opportunities that turn into deals. Global mindset. Hyper-local execution.

We’re looking for a driven Junior Recruiter to join our growing team and own the full-cycle recruitment process, partnering closely with executive leaders at Bisnow. In this role, you’ll proactively source top talent, build and manage new business relationships, and connect candidates with career-advancing opportunities. This is a unique opportunity for an ambitious professional to launch and grow a career within the Commercial Real Estate industry while being part of a thriving, entrepreneurial, and highly collaborative virtual business. This is a hybrid role based out of our NYC office at 123 William St. You’ll be in-office 3-4 days per week.

Key Responsibilities


Source and identify qualified candidates using a variety of recruiting tools and platforms
Screen applicants, conduct interviews, and complete reference checks
Present and communicate offers to selected candidates
Build and maintain a strong pipeline of talent aligned with key industry skill sets
Develop close relationships with candidates, guiding them toward their next career move while partnering with internal leadership to understand client needs
Proactively generate new business opportunities and leads
Engage with leading companies and executives across the Commercial Real Estate industry
Support the growth of Bisnow’s culture, community, and Diversity & Inclusion initiatives as part of the People Ops team, contributing to special projects and reinforcing company values
Partner with Hiring Managers to provide guidance and onboarding support, ensuring alignment with recruiting processes and systems



WHAT ARE WE LOOKING FOR?


Bachelor’s degree required (Communications, Psychology, Business preferred)
0 - 2 years of direct recruiting experience
Team-player, strong work ethic, no task is too small mentality
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Attention to detail and strong organizational skills
Quick learner, who enjoys acquiring knowledge in new subject areas
Creative thinker who is analytically capable and intellectually curious
Appetite, appreciation, and a genuine interest in the Commercial Real Estate Industry
Prior Real Estate experience is not required, but a bonus



WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU?


Competitive compensation structure!
Medical, Dental and Vision Insurance - effective the 1st of the month following your start date
Short and Long Term Disability Insurance - this includes maternity and paternity leaves as well.
401K
Flexible Spending Account
Health Savings Account
Dependent Care Account
Unlimited Vacation Days
7 days paid sick leave
9 paid Holidays
Referral Bonus Program
Pet Friendly Offices
You’ll get to work with incredibly smart, passionate, driven, ambitious, kind, caring and mindful people and will rarely experience an overload of policies, bureaucracy or toxicity (the latter, we do not tolerate)



Get To Know Our Teams! < Click here for more info about Bisnow, Biscred & SelectLeaders!

Before applying, please read our values below. Our values describe and give insight into our culture. If you do not share the same values, this role will not be a mutually beneficial fit. If you do share these values and want to apply, we encourage you to do so.

Our values demand that we be curious, self-aware, fearless, consistent. We say yes to seemingly insurmountable challenges because we dare ourselves to push further. And we go the distance because we are individually and collectively entrepreneurial, always asking: Why can’t it be done? We find a way because we care more than the competition. We embrace team and disown ego. We are ruthlessly disciplined and unabashedly kind. We unreservedly challenge the status quo and vigorously fight for new levels of excellence. We believe the difference between good and great is exceptional communication. We obsess over creating value for our team and our customers. We push ourselves and our industry to be more inclusive, to champion diversity and to fight racism, gender bias and all forms of inequality. We never peak. We never quit. We never have excuses. We own our failures and we commit to being smarter and stronger because of them. We give our best today and even better tomorrow. We are here to win.

Bisnow will not offer sponsorship for employment status (including, but not limited to, H1-B visa status and other employment-based nonimmigrant visas) for this position. Accordingly, all applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis and must not require Bisnow's sponsorship to continue to work legally in the United States.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Plus bonus!

title: Junior Data Analyst - Business Intelligence, Regional Operations
description:
About The Team

Shopee’s Regional Operations constantly solving problems and introducing innovative solutions to improve platform usability and enhance end user experience for our buyers, sellers, and partners.

Our teams sit at the core of our business and collaborate with various departments to manage end-to-end e-commerce processes, and design scalable solutions to create impact across multiple markets. To do so, we deep dive into the fundamentals of each aspect and drive strategic initiatives to sharpen our policy, processes and features for greater product and operational excellence.

Job Description

Our Business Intelligence Data Analysts go beyond performance monitoring and report - they play a crucial role in conducting in-depth data-powered analysis and research that drive key business decisions across Regional Operations.

We’re looking for a hands-on, motivated individual who enjoys complex problem-solving and deriving data-backed solutions to help drive high-impact business changes.


Work closely with large datasets to build data visualisation and dashboards that inform strategic planning and sound business decisions
Understand functional and technical requirements to build reports that deliver actionable insights to key stakeholders
Build and maintain data infrastructure (data pipelines, aggregated data sets, reports, dashboards) to facilitate development of key metrics to measure efficiency/impact/outcomes of day-to-day operations
Conduct research and synthesise findings into plans, processes, and playbooks that are clear and lead to actionable insights
Work closely with internal stakeholders from cross-functional and product teams across multiple markets to build, test, and implement new processes and systems
Drive adoption of the team’s initiatives through strong collaboration and alignment of organisational needs with various stakeholders


Requirements


Bachelor’s degree in Business Analytics, Statistics, Engineering, Mathematics or Computer Science
Proficiency in SQL/Python
Experience with big data tools (e.g. Hadoop, Hive SQL, Spark), ETL tools, data visualisation tools (e.g. Tableau, Looker) and statistical analysis (e.g. hypothesis testing, regression, data modeling techniques) preferred
Ability to analyse and synthesise data from both quantitative and qualitative sources
Ability to draw insights from data to to design practical and scalable solutions
Ability to explain technical concepts in a clear and succinct manner to various stakeholders
Ability to thrive in a dynamic and collaborative environment

title: Junior Valuation Analyst
description:
Bracebridge Capital, LLC is a leading alternative asset manager with approximately $12 billion of net assets under management. The firm pursues investment strategies primarily within the global fixed income markets with the objectives of capital preservation and absolute return without significant correlation to equity, interest rate and foreign exchange markets. Established in 1994, Bracebridge manages private investment funds that serve endowments, foundations, pension funds and other institutional and high-net-worth investors.

Approximately 160 employees operate from our office located in Boston’s historic Back Bay. The entrepreneurial and collaborative culture at Bracebridge rewards and supports motivated, dedicated, enthusiastic and intellectually curious individuals. We believe our firm’s greatest asset is the people who work here.

Bracebridge is currently seeking a financial markets professional with security and derivative pricing expertise in the global securitized markets, including subprime RMBS, agency and non-agency CMBS, structured bonds and other fixed and floating ABS. The Junior Valuation Analyst will work closely with the Valuation Manager as well as the firm’s traders and analysts throughout the valuation process.

Responsibilities


Evaluate the market prices used in securitized benchmark indices and other fixed income products and services on a daily, weekly and monthly basis. Analyze internally sourced pricing as well as externally sourced pricing for accuracy
Research and resolve pricing discrepancies through modeling and analysis and working with third party vendors
Work with Valuation Manager and other analysts to enhance pricing models. Conduct tolerance testing of results
Assist with the preparation of documentation for month-end reporting and regulatory audits


Requirements


Master's degree in finance, mathematics or a related field
Up to 2 years of “hands on” experience in a fixed income security valuation role
Knowledge of fixed income securities such as corporate bonds, RMBS, CMBS and ABS as well as of single name derivatives and indices
General understanding of traditional mortgage financing, yield curves and spread movements
Experience working with third party vendors such as Bloomberg, Markit, ICE, Intex and Pricing Direct
Excellent Excel skills
VBA, SQL and Python preferred
Ability to manage multiple tasks, prioritize effectively, meet deadlines and deliver high quality, error-free work in a fast-paced environment
Strong oral and written communication skills are essential
CFA, or in pursuit of a CFA, a plus


Current anticipated annual base salary range: $85,000 - $95,000

Base salary within the range will be determined by various factors including but not limited to the individual's experience, skills and qualifications.

title: Junior Front End Developer
description:
Description

Footasylum is a leading UK-based retailer specialising in streetwear and sportswear, committed to delivering innovative and trend-setting products to our customers, with a strong focus on digital transformation and data-driven decision-making.

We’re seeking a motivated Junior Front-End Developer to join our team. This entry-level role is perfect for recent graduates, career changers, or self-taught developers passionate about creating high-quality, user-friendly web experiences. You’ll collaborate closely with engineers, designers, and product teams to build responsive and accessible web applications.

The Team

The UX and Delivery team is a tight-knit, agile group responsible for a wide range of digital touchpoints across the business. We work closely together to support long-term digital delivery plans, CRO strategies, and daily BAU activities. While we collaborate regularly, each team member also works independently with strong communication skills and a shared commitment to design excellence.

About You

We are looking for someone who embodies the Footasylum values – Community, Collaborate and Challenge.

You will be familiar with responsive design and cross-browser compatibility

You will have a basic understanding of Git (commits, branching) and familiarity with platforms like GitHub

You will have a solid understanding of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript (ES6+)

Along with;


Exposure to a modern framework such as React (preferred), Vue, or similar
Awareness of web fundamentals like accessibility and performance
Strong willingness to learn and grow in a collaborative environment
Good problem-solving and communication skills


Desirable Skills


Experience with TypeScript
Basic knowledge of APIs (REST) and how front-ends consume data
Exposure to Node.js or full-stack concepts
Familiarity with CSS frameworks or tools (e.g. Tailwind CSS)
Understanding of testing basics (e.g. unit/UI testing)
Core: HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript (ES6+)
Framework: React
Styling: Tailwind CSS / modern CSS (Flexbox, Grid)
Tools: Git, npm/yarn, Vite or similar build tools
Design: Figma
APIs: REST
A/B testing & experimentation tools (e.g. Monetate)


Responsibilities


You will build and maintain responsive web interfaces using modern front-end technologies.
You will collaborate with designers and developers to implement new features.
You will translate UI/UX designs (e.g. from Figma) into clean, functional code.
You will learn and apply best practices in performance, accessibility, and SEO.
You will support the team in debugging, testing, and improving existing features.
You will contribute to component libraries and design systems.
You will stay updated on modern front-end tools and trends.


Why Footasylum?

We are one of the UK’s leading omni channel retailers, but as well as that, we’re a genuinely great place to work. We value you and your development. Our people across the business have taken on new opportunities, whether that’s stepping into a new role, moving into a different department, or building new skills through the support and training we offer. Our goal is to create a fun, supportive environment where your success matters just as much as ours, and where you have the tools, encouragement, and opportunities to achieve your goals.

Diversity

We recognise and value the importance of diversity to help make sure we have a diverse perspective when we are building services to customers and the wider business. This is great news for us. But for us, diversity is also about, building happy energised teams full of people who want to learn and want to be inspired by each other's different backgrounds and experiences.

Recruitment Process

We review applications on an individual basis, and if we feel you would be a good fit we’ll invite you for a call or Teams video for an informal chat about the role, and to see if we’re a good fit for you.

We value open and honest conversations and collaboration, giving you a chance to learn about what we are doing in an informal and friendly environment. We want to know about you and why you feel that this is the opportunity for you. Please note, this is not a remote role and our expectation is that you will be able to attend Head Office in a hybrid way, in Greater Manchester.

title: GUCCI Junior Product Developer
description:
Summary

About Gucci
Influential, innovative and progressive, Gucci is reinventing a wholly modern approach to fashion. Under the new vision of Artistic Director Demna, the House has redefined luxury for the 21st century, further reinforcing its position as one of the world’s most desirable fashion houses. Eclectic, contemporary, romantic — Gucci products represent the pinnacle of Italian craftsmanship and are unsurpassed for their quality and attention to detail. Gucci is part of the Kering Group, a world leader in apparel and accessories that owns a portfolio of powerful luxury and sport and lifestyle brands.

Job Description

Role Mission 

The Junior Product Developer will support the daily operations of the Creative Atelier and the Product Development team, supporting the smooth execution of both creative and technical activities. In this hybrid operational and development-focused role, you will contribute to the development of RTW collections, assist during fittings, and update all related documentation. You will also manage essential atelier logistics, including supplies, shipments, and IT support requests— to ensure an efficient and well-organized workflow. Working closely with the Senior Product Developer, internal departments, suppliers, and General Services, you will help maintain continuity across all stages of product development, from prototype creation to collection delivery.

Responsibilities


Manage the ordering and inventory of materials for the Creative Atelier ensuring continuous availability for tailoring activities.
Oversee the procurement and organization of office supplies, ensuring adequate stock and smooth operations.
Facilitate the creation of IT support tickets with Kering and Lectra for computer-related issues or the setting up of profiles for new hires.
Assist in handling requests for courier services and other messenger-related tasks.
Collaborate with General Services for any requests related to ordinary and extraordinary resources.
Support in attending fittings and taking notes to update documentation.
Assist in the preparation of documentation necessary for the transfer of information between pattern makers and the design team.
Organize and maintain files containing fitting information and collection details.
Support in preparing documents and project delivery timelines.


Key Requirements


Previous experience (1–2 years) in a similar role, ideally within RTW development for luxury or premium brands.
Ensure accuracy in expense reporting, shipment monitoring, and cataloging accessories.
Strong organizational and multitasking skills.
Comfortable using IT systems and creating support tickets for technical issues.
Proactive and willing to learn in a fast-paced environment
Strong ability to liaise with internal teams and external vendors for smooth operations.
Flexible to support activities and respond to urgent transportation or logistics needs.
Availability to travel occasionally within Italy and abroad.
Fluency in Italian and good command of English, both written and spoken.
Good knowledge of Excel and any relevant software packages.


Why work with us?

Gucci is committed to building a diverse workforce. We believe diversity in all its forms – gender, age, nationality, culture, religious beliefs and sexual orientation – enriches the workplace. It opens up opportunities for people to express their talent, both individually and collectively and it helps foster our ability to adapt to a changing world. As an Equal Opportunity Employer we welcome and consider applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of their background.

description:
Job Description

As an Estimator II here at Honeywell, you will be responsible for developing accurate and competitive cost estimates for various projects. This role is essential in ensuring the success and profitability of our bids and supporting the growth of our organization.

In this role, you will impact the accuracy and competitiveness of our project bids, ensuring that they align with customer expectations and company standards. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather project requirements and provide detailed cost estimates that support our business objectives.

Your role will also include overseeing, supervising and reviewing tasks performed by team members to ensure effective execution of work; managing end‑to‑end processes and projects for both internal and external clients with responsibility for timely and accurate delivery; issuing clear instructions and directions to team members on tasks to be performed; and mentoring and guiding junior colleagues to support their skill development, professional growth, and overall success

Responsibilities

Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather project requirements and develop cost estimates
Analyze project plans, specifications, and drawings to determine project scope and identify potential risks
Prepare detailed cost estimates, including labor, materials, equipment, and subcontractor costs
Review and validate subcontractor and vendor quotes to ensure competitiveness and accuracy
Support the estimation process for medium to large-scale projects
Assist in bid review meetings and present cost estimates to senior management


Qualifications

YOU MUST HAVE


2+ years of experience in estimating
Strong knowledge of construction methods, materials, and costs
Proficiency in using estimating software and tools
Ability to interpret project plans, specifications, and drawings


WE VALUE


Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or a related field
Attention to detail and accuracy
Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
Continuous learning and professional development mindset


About Us

Honeywell helps organizations solve the world's most complex challenges in automation, the future of aviation and energy transition. As a trusted partner, we provide actionable solutions and innovation through our Aerospace Technologies, Building Automation, Energy and Sustainability Solutions, and Industrial Automation business segments – powered by our Honeywell Forge software – that help make the world smarter, safer and more sustainable.

Our Client

Our subsidiary CF Invest, is a dedicated recruitment firm focused on positions in the investment sector.




We are recruiting on behalf of our client, one of the bulge bracket bank and pre-eminent investment banks.




This tier one firm works with us to select the highest calibre talent in the global markets at entry and experienced entry level across their global offices on an ongoing basis through the first half of each year, with the view of onboarding immediately or upon graduation.

The role requires talent to advise clients on various financial transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, equity/equity-linked and debt financings, joint ventures, restructurings, and financial investments.







Your Focus

As an Investment Banking Analyst, your focus is on supporting clients through complex capital markets and advisory transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, and capital raising. You will work closely with senior bankers to analyse opportunities, structure transactions, and help clients make informed strategic decisions.




The role is execution-driven and detail-intensive. You will build and maintain financial analyses, support transaction execution, and contribute to client materials used in live deals. You will also help assess risk, ensure adherence to regulatory and compliance requirements, and maintain the highest standards of accuracy, judgement, and professionalism.




This role suits someone who is analytically rigorous, commercially aware, and motivated by working at pace on high-stakes transactions. You will be expected to take ownership of your work, communicate clearly with colleagues and clients, and contribute meaningfully to deal delivery in a demanding, regulated environment.

Support the execution of investment banking transactions, including M&A, capital raising, and strategic advisory mandates

Assist senior bankers in coordinating transaction workstreams and managing timelines

Contribute to client development activities through research, analysis, and preparation of marketing materials
Assess transaction-related risks and support adherence to regulatory, compliance, and internal control requirements

PayPal has been revolutionizing commerce globally for more than 25 years. Creating innovative experiences that make moving money, selling, and shopping simple, personalized, and secure, PayPal empowers consumers and businesses in approximately 200 markets to join and thrive in the global economy.

We operate a global, two-sided network at scale that connects hundreds of millions of merchants and consumers. We help merchants and consumers connect, transact, and complete payments, whether they are online or in person. PayPal is more than a connection to third-party payment networks. We provide proprietary payment solutions accepted by merchants that enable the completion of payments on our platform on behalf of our customers.

We offer our customers the flexibility to use their accounts to purchase and receive payments for goods and services, as well as the ability to transfer and withdraw funds. We enable consumers to exchange funds more safely with merchants using a variety of funding sources, which may include a bank account, a PayPal or Venmo account balance, PayPal and Venmo branded credit products, a credit card, a debit card, certain cryptocurrencies, or other stored value products such as gift cards, and eligible credit card rewards. Our PayPal, Venmo, and Xoom products also make it safer and simpler for friends and family to transfer funds to each other. We offer merchants an end-to-end payments solution that provides authorization and settlement capabilities, as well as instant access to funds and payouts. We also help merchants connect with their customers, process exchanges and returns, and manage risk. We enable consumers to engage in cross-border shopping and merchants to extend their global reach while reducing the complexity and friction involved in enabling cross-border trade.

Our beliefs are the foundation for how we conduct business every day. We live each day guided by our core values of Inclusion, Innovation, Collaboration, and Wellness. Together, our values ensure that we work together as one global team with our customers at the center of everything we do – and they push us to ensure we take care of ourselves, each other, and our communities.

Strong written and verbal communication skills

PayPal

title: Associate Engineer, NOC
description:
hackajob is collaborating with Scale Computing to connect them with exceptional professionals for this role.

Who We Are

Acumera is now Scale Computing! Scale Computing is a global leader in edge computing, hyperconverged infrastructure, and managed networking solutions. We deliver innovative, secure, andscalable technology that powers critical operations worldwide.

About The Role

Trainee/Associate Engineer will oversee daily operation of support desk functions that includes taking calls as first level support, entering the reported problems in the ticketing system, solving user problems, proper escalation of open problems and dealing with the customers. Will also participate in preventative maintenance, troubleshooting, configuring and providing technical

support for end-users.

Great Candidates Will Look Like This

A team player who can work well within the team and collaborate cross-functionally, especially in a remote environment.

An excellent communicator who is efficient and effective with customers and colleagues via instant messages, email, video conferencing and in-person interactions.

Enjoy troubleshooting and problem-solving analytically.

Essential duties and responsibilities include the following: (Other duties may be assigned.)

Provide helpful and professional assistance to customers via phone, email and tickets.

Create or update support tickets as required for phone calls.

Document all calls, actions, and any relevant information in ticket notes.

Handle tickets created by customers via email.

Follow up on existing tickets proactively and within specified time frames.

Resolve or escalate tickets in a timely manner, within SLA guidelines.

Interact with other departments and teams, collaborating on tickets or solutions.

Evaluate customers’ needs and determine the appropriate action.

Troubleshoot complex technical issues with customers via phone.

Understand and follow security and other guidelines for customers’ accounts.

Process voicemails, creating or updating support tickets as necessary.

Write and send customized email responses to customers for specific situations.

Understand and adhere to Scale Computing policies and procedures.

Place outbound calls to resolve internally-detected issues.

Be professional and courteous at all times, especially when speaking or conversing with customers.

Technical Requirements

Knowledge of at least some of the following:

General computer systems

PCs, laptops, mobile devices.

Current and recent Operating Systems

Windows, Mac OS, Linux

Routing Protocols and concepts

Static and Dynamic routing, Distance, Priority, routing table, Default Gateway

Switching Protocols and concepts

VLAN, Trunks and link bonding, Spanning Tree and RSTP, MAC Address table,

ARP.

Networking Protocols and concepts

TCP, UDP, DHCP, DNS, VPN, ICMP, TELNET, SSH FTP, HTTP(s), IPSEC, SSL,etc.

Network troubleshooting tools such as PING, TRACEROUTE, NSLOOKUP, IPCONFIG, etc.


OSI Model – Advanced understanding of layers 1-4
Subnetting and Supernetting
Firewall rules and policies, Access Lists


Deep understanding of technical concepts within networking environments as well as security, risk and compliance.

Entry Level knowledge of Switches, Routers, Firewalls, and Networking in general is required. Must be detail-oriented and must be able to work well in a team environment.

Reporting Structure

Reports to Tier 1 Manager - NOC.

Education And Experience

Bachelor’s degree (B.E./B.Tech/BCA) in a technical domain such as Computer Science, Telecommunication, Electronics & Communication, Information Technology, or a related field.

Minimum experience in the networking domain is preferred. (0-2 years)

Experience Working In Helpdesk Or Technical Support Is Preferred.

Industry certifications like CompTIA Network+, CCNA, CCNP, JNCIA, Wireshark, etc. are preferred.

Perks of Acumera

Group medical insurance.

Paid company holidays.

Casual dress code

Cab facilities for employees working in shifts.

Fully stocked snacks at office.

Vibrant and Inclusive Workplace Atmosphere.

Flexible work environment and an opportunity to grow as we grow.

Scale Computing is an equal-opportunity employer. All final candidates will be subject to a pre-employment background check.

title: Investment Banking Summer Analyst
description:
This role is for the Bengaluru office. Those across India can apply.







Our Client

Our subsidiary Global Markets Recruitment (“GMR”) is a dedicated recruitment firm focused on emerging and developed markets at entry to middle-management level.




We have been asked to asked by our client to run a campaign to identify entry level interns for this summer 2026. Our client is a bulge bracket bank and one of the pre-eminent investment banks.




This tier one firm works with us to select the highest calibre talent in the global markets at entry and experienced entry level across their global offices. We will run campaigns for them on an ongoing basis through the first half of this year.




Our client is looking for people in their global offices and we will post these roles for each location. Please apply to the location closest to you.




You will either be placed in their FIG or TMT Group or possibly another division as they also seek cross industry people also in energy, Industrials, Healthcare and Consumer.




The role requires talent to advise clients on various financial transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, equity/equity-linked and debt financings, joint ventures, restructurings, and financial investments.







Your Focus

As an Investment Banking Analyst - Intern, your focus is on supporting clients through complex capital markets and advisory transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, and capital raising. You will work closely with senior bankers to analyse opportunities, structure transactions, and help clients make informed strategic decisions.




The role is execution-driven and detail-intensive. You will build and maintain financial analyses, support transaction execution, and contribute to client materials used in live deals. You will also help assess risk, ensure adherence to regulatory and compliance requirements, and maintain the highest standards of accuracy, judgement, and professionalism.




This role suits someone who is analytically rigorous, commercially aware, and motivated by working at pace on high-stakes transactions. You will be expected to take ownership of your work, communicate clearly with colleagues and clients, and contribute meaningfully to deal delivery in a demanding, regulated environment.







Responsibilities

Support the execution of investment banking transactions, including M&A, capital raising, and strategic advisory mandates
Build and maintain detailed financial models and analyses to evaluate transactions and strategic options
Prepare client-facing materials, including pitch decks, information memoranda, and transaction documentation
Conduct company, industry, and market research to support deal origination and execution
Assist senior bankers in coordinating transaction workstreams and managing timelines
Support due diligence processes, including data analysis and coordination with internal and external stakeholders
Contribute to client development activities through research, analysis, and preparation of marketing materials
Assess transaction-related risks and support adherence to regulatory, compliance, and internal control requirements
Maintain high standards of accuracy, organisation, and confidentiality across all work
Work effectively with colleagues across banking, markets, legal, risk, and compliance teams




This role is about supporting the execution of complex investment banking transactions and helping clients make informed strategic and capital decisions.




You can absorb detailed financial and commercial information quickly, analyse companies, transactions, and market conditions with rigour, and form clear, well-reasoned conclusions. You approach problems with structure and discipline, and you are comfortable working under pressure as deals progress and priorities evolve.







Minimum Qualifications

Graduate – Analyst

At university studying any degree ideally in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline. We accept also individuals from any degree, especially those numerate based including Scientific, Engineering, Arts etc
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear client-ready materials
High attention to detail and strong organisational skills
Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities
Sound judgement and a strong understanding of risk, compliance, and regulatory expectations







Process

This application shall be open until we reach a cap of applicants, so please apply early. We are hiring for our client across their key global markets. Thereafter once selected, you will undergo our client one day assessment centre four interviews and one group role play. A decision will be made on the day.




Be sure to Follow Us on LinkedIn for updates on this vacancy and others: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cfcorp




Your first step is to apply with your LinkedIn profile and CV. We do not need a covering letter. Due to the volume of applications we receive, we are unfortunately unable to provide individual feedback.







Reward Structure

You will be well compensated for this time and the intern program will last for six weeks. We are running three programs from mid-June, end June and mid-July
Receive a bonus for your time
A permanent offer of employment and part of the Banks’ graduate program
Relocation is provided by the company. Starting salaries will be paid in USD for India $125,000 and up to $150,000 depending on experience
Bonus program of on-target bonus of 50% of base salary and up to 125% for high-achievers.

title: Business Development & Growth Associate – Fintech / Supply Chain Finance
description:
Job Title: Business Development & Growth Associate – Fintech / Supply Chain Finance

Experience: 1–3 years

Location: Bangalore (Onsite)

Employment Type: Full-Time




The Opportunity

Our client, a trade technology startup backed by a 40-year-old business house with consolidated revenues of over ₹20,000 crore, is looking for a driven and curious Business Development Associate to support revenue growth, enterprise outreach, and partnership development. This is an entry-to-mid-level role that

sits at the intersection of growth, finance, technology, and supply chain




You will work alongside senior BD and leadership teams to execute outreach strategies, qualify prospects, support deal solutioning, and help build the client’s presence across target accounts and partner ecosystems.




This is ideal for someone who is curious about trade and structured finance, eager to learn the business end-to-end, and energized by the prospect of building client relationships from the ground up.




What You’ll Own

Prospecting & Outreach

Research and identify target accounts across sectors such as manufacturing, FMCG, pharma, logistics, and retail
Execute multi-channel outreach including cold emails, cold calls, LinkedIn outreach, and structured mailer campaigns
Qualify inbound and outbound leads and schedule discovery calls for senior BD team members
Maintain and update prospect data, outreach logs, and pipeline status in the CRM on an ongoing basis




Sales Support & Deal Assistance

Support the full sales cycle from initial outreach through to discovery, solutioning, and follow-up
Prepare and assist in meeting briefs, client-facing decks, and solution summaries 
Take and circulate accurate meeting minutes and action items post client interactions
Assist in preparing proposals, term sheets, and onboarding documents




Partnerships & Ecosystem Development

Support outreach and relationship-building with banks, NBFCs, fintech platforms, and logistics providers
Assist in tracking partnership conversations, follow-ups, and co-GTM opportunities




Pipeline, CRM & Reporting Discipline

Maintain a clean, up-to-date pipeline in CRM tools (Zoho, Salesforce, or equivalent)
Ensure consistent hygiene across deal stages, contact records, and communication logs
Pull pipeline and outreach reports to support team reviews and forecasting




Market Research & Competitive Intelligence

Track developments in trade finance, supply chain credit, and working capital markets
Identify and flag relevant triggers such as fundraises, leadership changes, earnings stress, or supply chain disruptions that signal prospect readiness




Thought Leadership & Events Support

Assist in representing the client at industry events, webinars, and ecosystem forums
Support content and positioning efforts including mailers, LinkedIn posts, and outreach messaging




What You Bring

1–3 years of experience in one or more of the following:
B2B sales, enterprise sales, or business development
Banking, trade finance, fintech, or supply chain finance
Inside sales, sales development, or outbound prospecting
Demonstrated ability to drive outreach and open conversations with senior stakeholders
Strong interest in finance, working capital, and structured products, prior exposure is a plus, but a genuine desire to learn is equally valued
Comfort with high-volume outreach, follow-ups, and working in a fast-paced, target-oriented environment




Core Skills

Clear and confident communication, written and verbal
Organized and detail-oriented, with strong follow-through on commitments
Ability to research, synthesize, and present information concisely
Comfortable working across multiple accounts and priorities simultaneously
A collaborative mindset with the hustle to operate independently when needed




Tools & Systems

Working knowledge of CRM tools such as Zoho, Salesforce, or equivalent is expected
Prior hands-on experience with sales intelligence and prospecting tools, Apollo.io, Lusha, RocketReach, or LinkedIn Sales Navigator, is preferred
Familiarity with email outreach sequencing tools is a plus
Basic proficiency in spreadsheets and presentation tools




Preferred Experience

Prior exposure to trade finance, supply chain finance, working capital products, or structured credit, whether in a sales, product, operations, or client servicing capacity
Experience or strong familiarity with structured finance products including invoice discounting, channel finance, supply chain credit, or sales bill discounting
Background in fintech, NBFCs, B2B SaaS, or financial services
Experience working with or prospecting into large corporates, conglomerates, or MNCs
MBA or equivalent from a reputed institution (preferred, not required)




What You’ll Get

Competitive fixed compensation with performance-linked variable
Direct exposure to complex enterprise deal-making and structured finance from day one
Mentorship from leadership with backgrounds at McKinsey, EY and Flipkart
High ownership, fast learning curve, and a seat at the table in a growing fintech
A high-trust, high-accountability culture where your work directly shapes outcomes

Businesses worldwide trust our expertise to protect their assets, relying on our comprehensive risk assessments and robust, engineering-based insurance solutions to safeguard against fire, natural disasters, and other perils. Serving over a quarter of the Fortune 500 and major corporations globally, we deliver data-driven strategies that enhance resilience, ensure business continuity, and empower organizations to thrive.

FM India is a strategic location for driving our global operational efficiency. Our presence in India allows us to leverage the country’s talented workforce and advance our capabilities to serve our clients better. We have diverse corporate functions that emphasize research, advanced technologies like AI and analytics, risk engineering, research, finance, marketing, HR, etc. working together to provide innovative solutions and nurture lasting relationships – from co-workers to clients.




Role Title: Data Engineer

Position Summary:

The Data Engineer is a key role on the Data Analytics team. The Data Engineer is responsible for developing, maintaining, and supporting data pipelines, data models, and data assets that enable analytics, reporting, and downstream data consumption. This role focuses on implementing well defined data solutions using approved technologies, following established standards for data quality, security, and reliability. The Data Engineer is responsible for creating and managing data infrastructure, data pipeline design, implementation and data verification. Along with the team, the Data Engineer is responsible for ensuring the highest standards of data quality, security and compliance. Additionally, the Data Engineer will implement methods to improve data reliability and quality, combine raw information from different sources to create consistent data sets. Incumbents are learning relevant technologies. Displays personal accountability for successful outcomes and support quality efforts within the team. Receptive and responsive to mentoring from more senior team members on design and development techniques, enhancements, or support efforts. The Data Engineer will need to become versed in DataOps, especially where Agile development, DevOps, and Continuous Improvement intersect. The Data Engineer I is the entry level position in the Data Engineer job family. Those holding this position are typically assigned to work as part of a project team.




Job Responsibilities:

Data Engineering & Development:

Develop working knowledge of structured data sources within each product journey (Underwriting and Risk; Client Service, Sales and Marketing; Claims; Account and Location Engineering).
Partner with Data Analytics team members, developers, solution architects, business analysts, data engineers, data analysts, data scientists to understand data and reporting needs.
Create and use data models as a means toward developing and documenting code.
Learn technologies such as Fabric Platform, Synapse Analytics Platform, Azure services, Kafka and others as required
Validate code through detailed and disciplined testing.
Participate in peer code review to ensure solutions are accurate .
Ensure tables and views are designed for data integrity, efficiency and performance, and are easy to comprehend.




Move and Store Data:

Contribute to the design, development, and maintenance of data pipelines and integrations for structured and unstructured data sources across enterprise product domains (e.g., Underwriting, Risk, Claims, Client Services, Sales and Marketing), working under the guidance of senior engineers.
Develop and support ETL/ELT solutions using approved cloud and analytics platforms (e.g., Synapse, SQL Server, Azure services such as Data Factory, Fabric, and data lakes), following established architectures, standards, and best practices.
Apply standard testing practices and participate in peer reviews to help ensure data solutions meet expectations for accuracy, performance, scalability, and maintainability, with coaching and feedback from more experienced team members.
Implement defined data flows and infrastructure pipelines to support the movement and storage of structured and unstructured data into and out of Data Analytics data assets, adhering to documented designs and operational procedures.
Design data models and data flows into and out of Data Analytics databases.
Understand and design data relationships between business and data subject areas.
Follow standards for naming conventions, code documentation and code review.




Data Quality, Reliability & Operations:

Investigate reported data quality issues, helping identify root causes and assisting with fixes under guidance from senior team members.
Assist with data profiling, cleansing, and transformation tasks in collaboration with analytics and business partners.
Monitor data pipelines and systems for basic performance and reliability issues, escalating concerns and supporting optimization efforts.
Help resolve production issues by following established validation, testing, and deployment procedures; provide routine production support.
Work with DBAs and teammates to support warehouse configuration, maintenance, and day to day operations.
Learn and apply foundational data modeling concepts, including understanding relationships between business and data subject areas.
Follow established standards for naming conventions, documentation, version control, and code review.




Collaboration & Delivery:

Collaborate with product owners, solution architects, business analysts, data analysts, data scientists, and other engineers to understand basic data and reporting requirements and help translate them into technical tasks.
Assist with data exploration and transformation activities under guidance from senior team members.
Support data cleansing efforts by helping identify and correct data issues.
Perform data profiling to help surface data anomalies and quality concerns.
Participate in hybrid agile delivery practices, including backlog refinement, task estimation, prioritization, and raising potential conflicts or risks.
Assist with data preparation tasks for analytics, reporting, and modeling.
Help support users and production data applications by responding to questions and troubleshooting issues.
Support developers, data analysts, and data scientists who need to access and work with data in the data warehouse.
Analyze reported data quality issues and assist in identifying potential root causes.
Monitor system performance and escalate opportunities for optimization to senior engineers.
Help monitor storage capacity and system reliability.
Assist in resolving production issues by following established validation, testing, and deployment processes.
Communicate clearly and professionally with users, teammates, and management.
Provide ad hoc data extracts and basic analysis to support tactical business needs.




Participate in effective execution of team priorities:

Assist in identifying assigned work and documenting tasks in the team backlog, following established intake processes.
Maintain and organize assigned tasks according to provided priorities and guidance from senior team members or leads.
Communicate potential conflicts or competing priorities to a lead or manager in a timely manner.
Support production data processes by helping investigate issues, performing routine checks, and escalating problems as needed.
Collaborate with product and analytics partners to stay informed of documented database or business process changes that may impact data interpretation, under guidance from the team.

Skill and Experience:

1 - 2 year of experience required to perform essential job functions.
Working knowledge of SQL and relational database concepts, including basic normalization and dimensional modeling.
Experience supporting or building data warehouses and/or data lakes.
Familiarity with cloud-based data platforms and services (e.g., Fabric, Synapse Analytics, Azure services, Kafka).
Programming experience using SQL and Python; exposure to structured and semi-structured data formats (e.g., JSON).
Foundational understanding of DataOps concepts such as testing, deployment, monitoring, and operational support.
Relational and nonrelational database theory.
Knowledge of Azure Cloud applications.
ETL/ELT pipeline design and build.
Programming languages SQL, Python, Spark, Kafka, Azure, JSON.




Must Have Skills:

SQL.
Python, Spark, Data lakes, Data warehouses.
Fabric Platform.
Ability to read and create data models.
ETL/ELT Pipeline development experience.




Good to Have Skills:

Preferred: Kafka, Synapse Analytics Platform.
Relational (3NF) and non-relational (Inmon/Kimball) database theory.
AI/LLM knowledge, experience preferred.
Python.

title: Scrum Master I
description:
Exhibits the fundamental qualities of a servant leader and demonstrates the fundamental principles and benefits of the Agile framework in an effort to improve team performance in the areas of quality, predictability, flow and velocity. Assists teams in meeting their delivery goals through enforcing team working agreements; communication techniques, coordination and cooperation; identifying and eliminating impediments; and ongoing coaching and development.

Acts as a servant leader for a single team and stakeholders. Aids the team and Product Owner in efforts to manage and sequence the backlog. Leads less complex teams with work contained with limited external team impact or dependencies. Works generally with internal systems involving few system integration points requiring occasional support from senior level Scrum Master. Eliminates impediments and resolves issues with frequent support from Release Train Engineer or other resources. Resolves and manages dependencies with frequent guidance from Release Train Engineer or other resources to support team improvement and promotes high performance. Manages team performance in the areas of quality, predictability, flow and velocity and identifies SAFe anti-patterns and resolve with frequent support. Utilizes beginner coaching capabilities to influence the team and help coach on practices and procedures. With frequent support from RTE guides the team through the decision making process and teach techniques on how to problem solve, improve communication, self-manage and self-organize. With frequent support from RTE Scrum Master, builds a trusting and safe environment where problems can be raised. Help manages interpersonal conflicts, challenges and opportunities for growth within the team. Participates, facilitates and manages the outputs of all agile ceremonies including daily Stand Up meetings, Sprint review, sprint r etrospective, sprint planning, backlog refinement sessions, prepare for Program Increment Planning Sessions. Participates in the Scrum Mastery Sync Sessions and Agile Release Train Sync Sessions. Demonstrates ongoing desire for knowledge and continuous improvement by pursuing continuous education with regards to everything Agile (e.g. visit user groups, attend conferences, read books, write blogs, etc.). Continuously works with the Scrum Master Community of Practice to ensure standard procedures are in place and ultimately improve the organizations' product delivery capability. Seeks advice and works closely with other Scrum Masters and Release Train Engineers to establish best practices and resolve challenging issues, risks, conflicts or impediments. Performs other duties as assigned.

Communication skills - Intermediate

Team Facilitation skills - Intermediate

Microsoft Tools - Entry Level (1-3 Years)

Position Summary

Finance Service Desk (FSD) - Associate Analyst/ Analyst

Deloitte believes in its diverse talent. That is why only the BEST choose to be at Deloitte. The FSD team is an internal support line for Deloitte employees for multiple Geographies for Business Application Support. We support all types of calls concerning problems with business applications. FSD team differentiates itself from other Call Centers by way of its work culture which encourages delivering distinctive service and the Rewards and Recognition programs which encourage taking initiative. FSD expects people to work with minimum supervision, think out of the box, make quick decisions, provide attention to detail and be flexible to change.

Work you’ll do

In this help desk position, you will be required to answer and resolve incoming calls, chats, webforms, Voicemails, emails & Fax from Deloitte employees situated globally. It might involve either direct resolution of the problem or escalation to another team.

tickets, chats and voicemail) with end users.

.

Adheres to Code of Ethics, Vision, Mission and Core Values.
Ability to switch between different countries and cultures (Americas, EMEA, APAC)

Excellent written, verbal, listening, analytical skills.

Knowledge of computer, Internet connectivity and application performance are required.

Excellent troubleshooting skills to diagnose and resolve/address customer issues/requests


Location : Hyderabad

title: Business Intelligence/Data Analytics Specialist
description:
India, Hyderabad

BUSINESS INTELLIGENCE/DATA ANALYTICS SPECIALIST

LOCATION: Hyderabad

Intro Summary Of The Role

At Lonza, we are looking for a Business Intelligence Analyst to join our Group Intelligence team. This role supports the technical delivery and day-to-day operation of Lonza’s Business Intelligence platforms, with a primary focus on Commercial, Sales, and Marketing use cases. You will contribute across the full BI lifecycle—from data ingestion and modelling through to analytics, applications, and user-facing reporting—ensuring BI products are reliable, scalable, and aligned to business needs.

What You Will Get

At Lonza, we offer a collaborative environment guided by our core values: Collaboration, Accountability, Excellence, Passion, and Integrity. You will benefit from professional growth opportunities, exposure to global teams, and the chance to shape BI solutions that directly impact business performance. (Region-specific benefits such as healthcare, retirement plans, and relocation support should be added here per the regional template.)

What You Will Do


Act as an active member of the Omni BI project team, supporting delivery and evolution of BI products.
Gather business requirements and scope solutions with a strong focus on end users.
Develop ETL processes, manage data integration and pipelines.
Design and deliver user-centric reports and dashboards with strong storytelling and usability principles.
Generate analytics and insights to support commercial decision-making.
Strengthen data foundations by improving data quality, availability, and traceability.
Provide operational support for BI platforms, including monitoring, issue resolution, and performance optimization.
Contribute to data governance through standards, metadata management, and access controls.
Identify opportunities for BI, analytics, or automation to deliver additional value, primarily within Marketing.
Encourage innovation through proof-of-concept development and continuous improvement of tools and processes.


What We Are Looking For


Education: Bachelor’s degree in Data, IT, Digital, or related fields.
Experience: Entry-level (0–4 years) in Data Analysis, Business Intelligence, or related areas.
Technical Skills:
Strong BI and data skills, with experience in database-driven environments.
Hands-on ETL experience with tools such as Snaplogic, SSIS, Azure Data Factory.
Awareness of data governance concepts (quality, lineage, controlled access).
Experience designing reports/dashboards using Power BI, Qlik, Tableau, Spotfire, Looker, or equivalent.
Advantageous: Experience with JavaScript frameworks (React, Angular, Vue, Node.js), containerized environments (Docker), CI/CD practices.
Ways of Working:
Experience in Agile or iterative delivery environments.
Strong analytical mindset, proactive, structured, and collaborative across technical and non-technical teams.

About Lonza

At Lonza, we put our people first. We aim to be a reference point for best practice, role-modelling our values in everything we do. Our work helps businesses deliver better outcomes through innovation, data-driven insights, and scalable solutions.

Lonza is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

title: Assistant Manager - Growth & Development (DLHP-MST-New Hire Learning) - Hyderabad
description:
Summary

Position Summary

Assistant Manager, Deloitte Leadership & Human Potential (DLHP)

Portfolio: New Hire Experience in Milestones, Social Impact & Transitions team (MST) based in Hyderabad

Learning and Development (L&D) at Deloitte aims to bring out the best of every person and the best of every team, so we can deliver the best of Deloitte to our clients. Our focus includes:


Bringing out the leader in every person
A holistic approach to development that emphasizes not just education, but also self-awareness, exposure to real-world problems, and connections to people
Positioning our people to help tackle some of the most complex issues in business, our communities and society at large.


The team

Deloitte Leadership and Human Potential (L&HP) is focused on delivering a leading-edge, branded learning experience across the firm, emphasizing human capabilities as complementary to technical ones and setting the standard for creating growth-oriented and inclusive professionals and leaders for the future of work. DLHP will include focus areas ranging from the humanities – integrating purpose; diversity, equity and inclusion; well-being; Enduring Human Capabilities (curiosity, informed agility, resilience, connected teaming, divergent thinking, and social and emotional intelligence)—to coaching and leading people and teams, communication skills, growth and sales, industry and market skills, and client leadership.

Work you’ll do

Supporting New Hire Experience space within the Milestones, Social Impact & Transition (MST),your primary focus is building and executing new hire onboarding development strategies, initiatives, and programs for new hires in the firm. In DLHP, your primary focus is to manage and develop curriculum development activities for new hire learning portfolio – leveraging SMRs and existing materials, and reviewing deliverables for accuracy and quality.

Strategy Development

10%

 Plan & execute a world class New Hire Onboarding experience strategy in USI while staying in constant touch with US team counterparts to maintain US-USI alignment

 Plan and lead curriculum development activities; leverage SMRs and existing materials; review deliverables for accuracy and quality.

 Conduct external research to find impactful leading-edge capability-building strategies, partnerships, and programs to support strategy implementation in the New Hire Learning & New Hire Onboarding space

Stakeholder Relationship Management

15%

 Build effective relationships with key stakeholders related to development projects. These range from Staff, Senior Staff, Managers, Senior Managers, SVPs, PPMDs, other USI LFHP team members, L&D SPOCs of other businesses, external empaneled vendor partners, US team members etc.

 Work closely with multiple teams like Talent Services, Office Experience, Regional Managing Principal’s office, Food & Beverage, Security, Operations etc. across the seven office locations to ensure smooth onboarding experiences.

 Participate in or lead project teams to build positive, collaborative partnerships among business stakeholders, and peers. Prepare draft presentations and reports to support stakeholder meetings.

 Maintain relationships with content-based leads (SMRs).

Operations Management

35%

 Identify areas for operational improvement, making suggestions and driving change to gain efficiencies and improve learning impact.

 Collaborate with Talent Services (Learning Development, Learning Operations, and Learning Delivery) to create and deliver leading-edge learning solutions.

 Execute assigned programs within budget and timeline.

 Run and analyze reports to provide preliminary insights on program success (Voice of Customer sentiment analysis).

 Manage all activities to be on-time and within budget, tracking against original plan to determine efficiency.

 Execute the CPE/certification process from objective mapping to form submission to participant receiving credits.

 Collaborate with Learning Operations to maintain accurate curriculum data integrity, maintain historical archives, and respond to ad hoc requests for curriculum information.

 Support cross-team operational initiatives as needed.

Curriculum Management

35%

 Work closely with the USI New Hire Experience (NHE) lead in carving out development and onboarding journeys for professionals in the region

 Collaborate with USI & US NHE team during weekly connects to highlight and address any India geo-specific content deviations and work towards achieving a consistent program design. Share content updates tailored to USI specific geography

 Consult with a USI NHE team to update content and modify the modalities of the DLaunch Program in response to organization-wide workforce transformation initiatives

 Own and execute end-to-end onboarding program experiences by understanding the brand these unique programs have built and be the brand ambassador of these programs by communicating the vision and purpose of these offerings

 Own and undertake quarterly / half-yearly Voice of Customer sentiment analysis by taking note of all feedback received during new hire onboarding across locations and suggesting location-specific Plan of Actions while keeping US NHE teams in sync

 Work hand-in-glove with NHE Lead to be the face of all New Hire Orientation programs in USI

 Appreciate the vision of MST team and then tailor onboarding learning experiences for new hires and develop learning offerings for NHE accordingly

 Support end-to-end participant journey, facilitator sourcing, and all related communications.

 Build on, and keep abreast of strong knowledge of development tools, modalities, and learning methodologies.

Resource Management & Development

5%

 Create a teaming environment which encourages sharing of best practices, driving innovation, and continuing improvement.

 Demonstrate positive team collaboration and inspire teaming culture that fosters individual and team growth—builds on strengths and showcases needle movement on developmental areas.

 Works closely with Talent Services Facilitators to identify gaps in resourcing for multiple DLaunch sessions across locations and provide inputs to solve for resourcing

 Complete and submit professional development and performance review documentation.

 Contribute towards an inclusive and high-performing teaming culture that maximizes team member contribution by creating an environment of mutual support and shared responsibility.

Qualifications

Required:


Bachelor’s Degree
At least 4-8 years of related professional experience
Collaborative mindset to share and implement best practices
Basic instructional design skills
Demonstrated project and program management experience: organization, managing details, keeping multiple tasks/projects on track
Ability to communicate effectively and credibly, both verbally and in writing, with audiences at all management levels
Ability to work autonomously and effectively in a fast-paced, complex, multi-tasking virtual environment
Excellent Microsoft Office skills
Up to 15% travel could be required based on role responsibilities


Preferred:


Experience working in a learning and development organization specifically on new hire onboarding programs
Prior consulting or professional services experience


Location: Hyderabad

From entry-level employees to senior leaders, we believe there’s always room to learn. We offer opportunities to help sharpen skills in addition to hands-on experience in the global, fast-changing business world.From on-the-job learning experiences to formal development programs, our professionals have a variety of opportunities to continue to grow throughout their career.

title: Associate Data Scientist (Data Engineering)
description:
Level: Associate (L3)

Role: Data Engineering Associate – MSO Ops

Mio Overview

The Marketing Science Operations (MSO) vertical at Amgen is the operational backbone that ensures accurate, timely, governed, and analytics ready marketing data across all digital channels. MSO enables the MIO (Marketing Investment & Optimization), and broader Decision Sciences teams to run self-service analytics, generate insights, and execute campaigns reliably.

MSO focuses on data readiness, governance, pipeline operations, vendor accountability, and automation with the goal of delivering clean, standardized, and insight-ready datasets.

Role Summary

The Associate – Data Engineering role is an entry‑level engineering position within the Marketing Science Operations (MSO) Data Engineering team. This role is ideal for candidates who are excited to build foundations in data pipelines, data quality, and scalable data products that power analytics and reporting.

The team will provide structured learning and on‑the‑job exposure. What matters most is curiosity, critical thinking, and a strong learning mindset.

LIVE | WHAT YOU WILL DO

As an MSO Data Engineering Associate, you will work under the guidance of Senior Associates and Managers to support data ingestion, transformation, and operational reliability.

You will:


Support building and maintaining data ingestion and transformation workflows (ETL/ELT) for marketing and media datasets
Assist with implementing data validations and QA checks (schema checks, null checks, duplication checks, count reconciliation) to ensure datasets are reliable and analytics‑ready
Help monitor pipeline runs and refresh schedules, investigate failures using logs, and escalate issues with clear context and evidence
Support development of reusable, governed datasets / data products that are consistent and easy for BI and analytics teams to consume
Contribute to documentation (data flow notes, runbooks, assumptions, source mapping) to improve operational stability and handoffs
Collaborate with cross‑functional partners (MSO BI, MSO Ops, Analytics teams, Vendors) to ensure inputs and outputs are aligned and dependable
Participate in continuous learning and improvement to strengthen engineering fundamentals, reliability practices, and delivery discipline
